combatNotes:
COMBOS & TACTICS (Barbarian 3 — Totem Warrior Bear):
• Rage first (Bonus Action) before entering melee — resistance to ALL non-psychic damage while Bear totem is active.
• Reckless Attack: advantage on all your STR attacks this turn, but enemies have advantage against you — acceptable when raging (halved damage).
• Greataxe base: 1d12+6 while raging (+4 STR, +2 rage). On crit: roll an extra d12 (Savage Attacks).
• Grapple combo: Reckless Attack for adv → hit → use second attack (Extra Attack at lvl 5, not yet) or grapple to restrain a target for allies.
• Danger Sense: advantage on DEX saves vs visible effects (traps, fireballs) — stay aware of what's in front of you.
• Speak with Animals (ritual only): interrogate wildlife for scouting info before combat.
• Heavy Crossbow: use only when melee isn't an option — no Rage damage bonus applies to ranged.
• Tip: enter Rage, charge the biggest threat, Reckless Attack. You are the damage sponge — protect the squishies.

feats.d: You can see in dim light within 60 feet as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.
When you are reduced to 0 hit points but not killed outright, you can drop to 1 hit point instead. Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a long rest.
When you score a critical hit with a melee weapon attack, you can roll one of the weapon's damage dice one additional time and add it to the extra damage of the critical hit.

On your turn, you can enter a rage as a bonus action. While raging: advantage on STR checks and saves, +2 bonus to melee damage, resistance to bludgeoning/piercing/slashing damage. Rage lasts 1 minute. 3 uses per long rest at level 3.
While not wearing armor, your AC equals 10 + your Dexterity modifier + your Constitution modifier. You can use a shield and still gain this benefit.

feats.s: Barbarian 3 — Path of the Totem Warrior
feats.d: While raging, you have resistance to all damage except psychic damage. The spirit of the bear makes you tough enough to stand up to any punishment.
You have advantage on Dexterity saving throws against effects that you can see, such as traps and spells. You must not be blinded, deafened, or incapacitated to gain this benefit.

When you make your first attack on your turn, you can decide to attack recklessly. Doing so gives you advantage on melee weapon attack rolls using Strength during this turn, but attack rolls against you have advantage until your next turn.

feats.s: Barbarian 3 — Path of the Totem Warrior
feats.d: You gain access to Bear totem spells. These spells are always prepared and don't count against the number of spells you can prepare. Speak with Animals (level 1–2), Beast Sense (level 3–4). You can cast them as rituals only.

You have an excellent memory for maps and geography, and can always recall the general layout of terrain, settlements, and other features around you. You can find food and fresh water for yourself and up to five other people each day, provided the land offers berries, small game, water, and so forth.

atks.no: Two-handed. +6 to hit. +2 dmg while raging (1d12+6).
Ammunition, heavy, loading, two-handed. +3 to hit.

1.desc: Speak with Animals — Conc. 10 min. You gain the ability to comprehend and verbally communicate with beasts.

2.desc: Beast Sense — Conc. 1 hour. Touch a willing beast. You can use your action to see through the beast's eyes and hear what it hears until the start of your next turn.

inv.no: Small decorative pin. Carried for unknown reasons.

rels.no: Rescued him in the ruins. Plays the flute. Light-fingered.

personality.traits: I care for my companions as if they were my cubs.
personality.bonds: The clan is what matters most.
